test_scenario: Low-value request routes to department manager only
preconditions: User is logged in as Maintenance requester; approval matrix is active; valid vendor and budget code exist
test_steps: 1. Create a standard Maintenance purchase request under CAD 1,000; 2. Enter required fields, valid budget code, and preferred vendor; 3. Submit the request; 4. Review routing and approval requirements
expected_result: Request routes to the Maintenance department manager only; finance review and executive approval are not required; manager decision comment and timestamp are available when approved
test_data: PR-2026-0052; synthetic low-value engine room gasket set under CAD 1,000
actual_result: Request routed to department manager only and approval comment was recorded

test_scenario: Medium-value request routes to manager and finance
preconditions: User is logged in as Maintenance requester; valid vendor and budget code exist; approval matrix is active
test_steps: 1. Create a standard Maintenance request between CAD 1,000 and CAD 9,999; 2. Submit the request; 3. Approve as department manager; 4. Review next workflow status and finance queue
expected_result: Request requires department manager approval plus finance review before procurement review; finance can record decision, hold reason, correction note, and completion
test_data: PR-2026-0021; synthetic medium-value parts request between CAD 1,000 and CAD 9,999
actual_result: After manager approval, request moved directly to procurement review instead of finance review

test_scenario: High-value request routes to executive sponsor
preconditions: User is logged in as Maintenance requester; valid vendor and budget code exist; approval matrix is active
test_steps: 1. Create a standard Maintenance request of CAD 10,000 or above; 2. Submit the request; 3. Approve as department manager; 4. Complete finance review; 5. Review executive sponsor queue
expected_result: Request requires department manager approval, finance review, and executive sponsor approval before procurement review; each decision is recorded with decision maker, timestamp, type, and comment
test_data: PR-2026-0037; synthetic high-value crane control panel repair over CAD 10,000

test_scenario: Missing budget code prevents request submission
preconditions: User is logged in as Maintenance requester; standard request is not marked emergency; budget code reference list is available
test_steps: 1. Create a standard Maintenance purchase request; 2. Complete all required fields except budget code; 3. Attempt normal submission; 4. Review validation message and workflow status
expected_result: Normal submission is blocked; the user receives a clear missing budget code correction message; the request does not route to manager, finance, or procurement unless an emergency safety exception applies
test_data: PR-2026-0111; synthetic Maintenance request with blank budget code
actual_result: Request submitted to manager queue with blank budget code and no blocking message

test_scenario: Invalid vendor places request on procurement hold
preconditions: Approved request exists; user is logged in as Procurement Coordinator; vendor reference data is available
test_steps: 1. Open an approved request with invalid, unknown, or non-preferred vendor details; 2. Attempt PO readiness review; 3. Select standardized procurement hold reason; 4. Save hold comment
expected_result: Request cannot proceed to PO created until vendor issue is reviewed; procurement hold reason and comment are recorded; status is visible to authorized users
test_data: PR-2026-0056; synthetic invalid vendor repair service request

test_scenario: Emergency safety request records fast-track justification
preconditions: User is logged in as Operations Requester; emergency safety option is available; department manager role exists
test_steps: 1. Create emergency safety purchase request; 2. Enter emergency justification and required available fields; 3. Submit through fast-track path; 4. Approve as department manager; 5. Review finance visibility and audit history
expected_result: Emergency request can continue through controlled fast-track only with justification; department manager approval is captured; finance visibility and post-approval audit notes remain required
test_data: PR-2026-0135; synthetic emergency safety ramp surface repair request

test_scenario: Rejected request requires reason and cannot create PO
preconditions: User is logged in as Department Manager; submitted request is pending approval
test_steps: 1. Open a pending request; 2. Attempt to reject without comment; 3. Enter rejection reason and save; 4. Open procurement PO-ready queue; 5. Open requester status view
expected_result: Rejection cannot be saved without a comment; rejected request is not eligible for PO creation; requester can see rejected status and business-facing rejection reason
test_data: PR-2026-0017; synthetic duplicate request rejection example

test_scenario: PO status records after required approvals complete
preconditions: User is logged in as Procurement Coordinator; request has required approvals, finance review when applicable, vendor details, quote status, and delivery instructions complete
test_steps: 1. Open approved PO-ready request; 2. Record PO handoff or PO status; 3. Enter PO reference, PO creation date, vendor contact status, and delivery instruction status; 4. Save and review request status
expected_result: Request status changes to PO Created; PO reference details are captured where available; unresolved finance or procurement holds would prevent PO-ready treatment
test_data: PR-2026-0041; synthetic approved Maintenance request moving to PO Created
actual_result: PO status recorded with reference and request status changed to PO Created

test_scenario: Receiving update remains separate from request status
preconditions: User is logged in as Receiving or Operations Staff; request is in ordering or delivery follow-up status
test_steps: 1. Open a request with PO status recorded; 2. Update delivery status to Partially Received; 3. Save receiving update; 4. Review request status, delivery status, and closure option
expected_result: Delivery status updates to Partially Received while request workflow status remains separate; request is not treated as fully fulfilled or closed until receiving is resolved and other holds are cleared
test_data: PR-2026-0027; synthetic partially received vendor service request

test_scenario: Status dashboard reflects latest workflow exceptions
preconditions: User is logged in as Executive Sponsor or Report Viewer; reporting extract is available with visible refresh timing
test_steps: 1. Open approval and exception dashboard; 2. Filter to Maintenance pilot; 3. Review backlog, finance holds, procurement holds, approval aging, and receiving status; 4. Compare dashboard counts to current synthetic request extract
expected_result: Dashboard shows backlog, aging, finance holds, procurement holds, cycle time, exception reasons, emergency requests, and receiving status with clear refresh timing
test_data: PR-2026-0111; synthetic finance hold status expected in reporting extract
actual_result: Dashboard extract did not include latest finance hold status after the displayed refresh timestamp

test_scenario: Duplicate request warning requires proceed reason or cancellation
preconditions: User is logged in as Maintenance requester; a similar recent request exists by category, vendor, amount range, and date window
test_steps: 1. Create a request similar to a recent request; 2. Submit the request; 3. Review duplicate warning; 4. Enter proceed reason or cancel submission; 5. Review procurement review notes
expected_result: Potential duplicate is flagged but not automatically blocked; user must provide a proceed reason or cancel; duplicate reason is available for procurement review and reporting
test_data: PR-2026-0118; synthetic duplicate request handling example

test_scenario: Approval notification is sent after workflow decision
preconditions: User is logged in as Department Manager; requester contact preference exists in synthetic role setup
test_steps: 1. Submit a complete request as requester; 2. Approve the request as manager; 3. Return to requester role; 4. Review notification or activity feed
expected_result: Requester is notified when the request is submitted and when approval decision is recorded; notification uses business-facing status language and links back to the request record
test_data: Synthetic Maintenance request with valid budget code and preferred vendor

test_scenario: Finance review records hold reason and completion
preconditions: User is logged in as Finance Reviewer; medium-value request is approved by department manager and awaiting finance review
test_steps: 1. Open finance review queue; 2. Select a request awaiting finance review; 3. Place request on finance hold with standardized reason and correction note; 4. Resolve hold and mark finance review complete
expected_result: Finance reviewer can record decision, hold reason, correction note, and review completion; procurement review remains blocked until finance issue is resolved or formally excepted
test_data: PR-2026-0121; synthetic medium-value request pending finance review

test_scenario: Role-based access limits restricted information
preconditions: Synthetic requester, manager, finance, procurement, executive, UAT tester, and report viewer roles exist
test_steps: 1. Log in as requester and open own request; 2. Attempt to view restricted finance notes and admin fields; 3. Log in as finance reviewer; 4. Open same request and review finance fields; 5. Log in as report viewer and open search results
expected_result: Permissions align to business responsibility; requester sees own business status but not restricted finance or admin details; finance and report roles see only information appropriate to their role
test_data: Synthetic role-based access examples; no real employee or financial data

test_scenario: Audit history captures decisions, holds, PO, and receiving updates
preconditions: User is logged in as UAT Tester or System Admin; request has submission, approval, finance, procurement, PO, and receiving events available
test_steps: 1. Open request audit history; 2. Review submission and edit events; 3. Review approval or rejection events; 4. Review finance hold, procurement hold, PO status, receiving update, comments, and closure events
expected_result: Authorized user can see relevant event, role or user type, timestamp, action, and comment for submission, edits, decisions, holds, PO updates, receiving updates, comments, and closure
test_data: Synthetic end-to-end request with full workflow history

test_scenario: Missing quote places request on procurement hold
preconditions: User is logged in as Procurement Coordinator; approved request requires supporting quote for PO readiness
test_steps: 1. Open approved request missing quote or supporting evidence; 2. Attempt PO readiness review; 3. Place request on procurement hold with Missing Quote reason; 4. Attach quote document; 5. Clear hold and review PO readiness
expected_result: Procurement can place request on hold with standardized missing quote reason and comment; attached quote is visible to authorized reviewers; request can continue only after PO readiness information is complete or formally excepted
test_data: PR-2026-0033; synthetic request pending procurement review with Missing Quote reason

test_scenario: Pending approval over SLA triggers follow-up visibility
preconditions: User is logged in as Project Manager or UAT Tester; approval SLA from approval matrix is active
test_steps: 1. Open requests pending manager approval; 2. Identify low-value request older than 2 business days; 3. Review overdue indicator; 4. Review requester or manager follow-up notification status; 5. Review approval aging reporting
expected_result: Request pending beyond SLA is visible as overdue; requester or manager follow-up notification is available; approval aging reporting reflects the overdue request
test_data: PR-2026-0113; synthetic low-value request pending manager approval beyond 2-business-day SLA

test_scenario: Authorized users search and filter procurement requests
preconditions: User is logged in as Project Manager or Procurement Coordinator; synthetic procurement request records exist
test_steps: 1. Open procurement request search; 2. Filter by request ID, department, category, vendor, amount band, status, priority, budget code, exception reason, and date range; 3. Review returned results; 4. Repeat with report viewer role
expected_result: Authorized users can search and filter by required fields; request workflow status remains separate from delivery status; restricted fields are hidden or limited by role
test_data: Synthetic request set including PR-2026-0029, PR-2026-0118, and PR-2026-0147

test_scenario: Vendor delay updates delivery status without closing request
preconditions: User is logged in as Procurement Coordinator or Receiving Staff; request has PO status or delivery follow-up started
test_steps: 1. Open request with delayed vendor response; 2. Record procurement hold or delivery delay reason; 3. Update delivery status to Delayed; 4. Review requester status view and reporting extract
expected_result: Vendor delay is visible using standardized hold or exception reason; delivery status shows Delayed separately from request workflow status; request is not closed until delivery or hold is resolved
test_data: PR-2026-0117; synthetic delayed vendor response request

test_scenario: Payment status is visible for received or closed requests
preconditions: User is logged in as authorized requester, manager, or report viewer; received and closed synthetic requests exist
test_steps: 1. Open received or closed request; 2. Review request status, delivery status, and payment status fields; 3. Filter reporting output for received or closed requests; 4. Confirm no payment action is available
expected_result: Received or closed requests show payment status visibility from the synthetic reporting extract where available; request status, delivery status, and payment status remain distinct; no payment approval or execution function is included
test_data: PR-2026-0043 and PR-2026-0120; synthetic received or closed requests with payment status values
